question: Which of the following statement/statements is/are valid regarding the office of the 'Whip'?

options.1: Although not mentioned in the Constitution of India, the office of the 'Whip' has however been incorporated in the Rules of the House by the Leaders and Chief Whips of Recognised Parties and Groups in Parliament (Facilities) Act, 1998.
options.2: The role of the Chief Whip of the government party in the Lok Sabha is served by the Minister of State for Parliamentary Affairs.
options.3: All political parties - whether ruling or opposition, will have their own Whips in the Parliament.
options.4: The Chief Whip is not responsible to the leader of the House but is responsible only to the leader of the party that has appointed him/her to the office of Chief Whip.
